title: Rocket-launch fire timer written to raw player+0x105C instead of last_z_trigger_timer (raw-cast sweep)
repro: Fire the rocket launcher (ITEM_ROCKETLAUNCH); the fire timer is written to player+0x105C (native mis-offset) instead of last_z_trigger_timer.

history.note: ASM-audit batch-6 raw-cast sweep, same class as FID-0085/0087/0088. Retail US ASM Weapon_function_grenades (7F064ED8: sw $t6, 0x105c($t8=g_CurrentPlayer)) stores the fire timer ($t6 = hand+0x20 = field_890) to g_CurrentPlayer+0x105C, followed by 'sw $t9=26, 0x24($s0)' (when_detonating_mines_is_0=26). N64 player+0x105C = last_z_trigger_timer (after field_101C Mtxf@0x101C). On the 64-bit port layout hands[] pointer growth shifts every post-hands field by a uniform +0x150 (test_struct_layout uniform-shift lock), so the raw write lands ~0x150 before last_z_trigger_timer (inside the hands[1] interior) AND leaves last_z_trigger_timer stale -- while the reader at gun.c:4419 (wor->timer = THROWN_ITEM_TIMER_DEFAULT - g_CurrentPlayer->last_z_trigger_timer) uses the named field. Fix: write g_CurrentPlayer->last_z_trigger_timer. Default ON; GE007_NO_LAST_Z_TRIGGER_TIMER_FIX restores the raw write byte-identically. Struct-layout lock: offsetof(struct player,last_z_trigger_timer) != 0x105C.
ASM-derived offset locked; see finding note.
One-line guard/field fix behind default-ON flag with byte-identical negative control (see finding note).

Task-specified validation bar GREEN: test_struct_layout PASS (field locks hold), sim_state_hash PASS, sim_invariance_gate PASS (dam1 f600 OFF==ON c83e78eabba5be2e), tape_regression PASS 7/7 byte-exact (zero sim-hash shift -- affected weapon/path not in any committed tape), ROM-free ctest green (only pre-existing out-of-tree intro_parse_digest CWD artifact). Negative control restores legacy raw offset byte-identically.
